title: Database Access on Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Database Access on Microsoft SQL Server
text: Database Access on Microsoft SQL Server You can access the database by configuring the logon path in the assignment file or when using the ProcScript open command. Logon Information In the assignment file, specify the driver and database path: [DRIVERS] MSS = Version [PATHS] $MSS MSS: DataSourceName :{ Database }|{ User }|{ Password } Copy Version —connector version identifier, for example U5.2. DataSourceName —configured ODBC data source name. Database —database name; optional if a default database is specified in the ODBC DSN (Data Source Name). User and Password —user and password as configured in the ODBC data source; optional—can be left empty if integrated security is used. From ProcScript, you can use the open statement: open " DataSourceName : Database|User|Password ","MSS" Copy Using MS-SQL as the Repository Microsoft SQL Server can be used for the Uniface Repository. To do so: Generate a SQL script from the command line using the /gensql /meta switch for the Microsoft SQL Server. For more information, see /genSql . Edit the script before running it by adding a suitable use database statement. Run the script from a SQL Workbench, for example, the Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio, to create the Repository tables and indexes. Note: The script generated by the /gensql switch does not contain definitions for triggers and stored procedures. In the dbms.asn file located in the /common/adm directory, modify the $IDF path to point to the MSS logon path. For example: [PATHS] $DBMS $MSS Copy Open/Close Support Both the open and close ProcScript instructions are fully supported. For example, if a default database has been defined for data source ROSE, you can specify the ProcScript statement open as follows, where as is the user name and db the password: open "ROSE:|sa|dba","mss" Copy Integrated security is assumed if you do not supply a user and password in the assignment file or the open ProcScript statement. For example, to log onto data source ROSE, database test with integrated security, you must specify the ProcScript statement open as follows: open "ROSE:test||","mss" Copy Loss of Connection If the SQLState is 08S01 (Communication link failure), indicating that the connection with the database has been lost, the MSS connector returns -9 in $procerror or $status .
